name: Ship Ready
description: Ship readiness is not about whether the feature works. It is about whether the artifact is ready for production: tests passing, coverage adequate, scope contained, changelog updated, version correct, and no outstanding hygiene debt. A feature that works but leaves the repo in worse shape is not ready to ship.

extras.text: What is the ship checklist? List every gate that must pass before this can merge: test command, coverage threshold, changelog entry, version bump, TODOS.md hygiene.

specSections.title: Ship Checklist
specSections.placeholder: _Gate checklist before merge: full test suite passes (zero failures), coverage at or above baseline, CHANGELOG updated with version, scope audit complete (built matches spec), no unresolved TODOs without tickets, version bump correct._

reminders: Tests must pass: run the full test suite (not just the new tests). Zero failures, zero skips on critical paths.
Coverage threshold: new code must meet or exceed the project's coverage threshold. Do not ship code that drops coverage below the project baseline.
Scope drift audit: compare what was built to what was specced. If features crept in or out, document why. Scope drift that is unacknowledged is a planning failure.
CHANGELOG entry: every user-visible change needs a CHANGELOG entry with the version it ships in. Breaking changes need a migration note.
TODOS hygiene: resolve or ticket every TODO added during this work. A merged TODO without a ticket is lost work.
Version readiness: is the version bump correct for the change type (patch/minor/major per semver)? Does the package.json / go.mod / deno.json reflect the new version?
acceptanceCriteria: Full test suite passes (zero failures, zero skips on critical paths)
Coverage at or above project baseline — not below
Scope audit complete: built matches spec, deviations documented
CHANGELOG updated with user-visible changes and version
Breaking changes have migration guide with before/after examples
No unresolved TODOs without associated tickets
Version bump correct for change type (patch/minor/major)
